name: Q19768331
abstract: Harold James Melvin (June 25, 1939 — March 24, 1997) was an American singer. He is best known for fronting Harold Melvin & the Blue Notes, who were regarded as pioneers in the Philadelphia sound of the 1970s.

concerts.label: Harold Melvin and The Blue Notes

about_quotes.speaker: Larry Gold
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee discusses the song 'Wake Up Everybody' and its writers (John Whitehead, McFadden) but does not mention any personal interaction, relationship, or direct knowledge of Harold Melvin himself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Larry Gold interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 18, 2025
